text.title: Problem Validation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Problem Validation application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Interviews use past-behaviour questions ('what did you do last time') not future-intent ('would you pay'). Future-intent answers are rejected as evidence.
rule.rationale: Future-intent answers over-state demand; past-behaviour reveals real pain.

rule.statement: Validation requires ≥7 interviews with the target segment. Single-segment, single-interview validation is rejected.
rule.rationale: <7 interviews give low confidence; 7 surfaces enough segment variation to surface failure modes.

rule.statement: Each interview documents the current workaround (paid tool / script / manual / none). Pains with strong existing workarounds drop confidence.
Report ends with a single verdict (GO / PASS / PIVOT) and a one-sentence rationale citing the weakest evidence row.
rule.rationale: Reports without verdicts get re-litigated; verdict pins the decision.

rule.statement: If operator pre-committed to building before validation, the methodology is skipped (skip-record emitted). Validation theatre is rejected.
rule.rationale: Validation that cannot return PASS is not validation; it produces false confidence.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
